6.3 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Background  Concurrent access （并发访问） to shared data may result in data inconsistency （数据不一致）  Maintaining data consistency requires mechanisms to ensure the orderly execution of cooperating processes （协作进程顺序执行）

# Page 4^page=4
6.4 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Background  Example ： Suppose that we wanted to provide a solution to the consumer-producer problem that fills all the buffers. We can do so by having an integer count that keeps track of the number of full buffers. Initially, count is set to 0. It is incremented by the producer after it produces a new buffer and is decremented by the consumer after it consumes a buffer.

# Page 5^page=5
6.5 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Producer Producer ： while (true) { /* produce an item and put in nextProduced*/ while (count == BUFFER_SIZE) ; // do nothing buffer [in] = nextProduced; in = (in + 1) % BUFFER_SIZE; count++; }

# Page 6^page=6
6.6 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Consumer Consumer ： while (true) { while (count == 0) ; // do nothing nextConsumed= buffer[out]; out = (out + 1) % BUFFER_SIZE; count--; /* consume the item in nextConsumed }

# Page 10^page=10
6.10 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Race Condition ( 竞争条件 )  What is a race condition?  Several processes access and manipulate the same data concurrently （原因：多个进程同时访 问相同数据）  The result of the execution depends on particular access order. （后果：执行结果与执行顺序有关）  To avoid race condition, we need to ensure that only one process at a time can be manipulating the same data.----mutual exclusion( 互斥访问 )

# Page 11^page=11
6.11 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Two threads, one counter Popular web server  Uses multiple threads to speed things up.  Simple shared state error:  each thread increments a shared counter to track number of hits  What happens when two threads execute concurrently? … hits = hits + 1; …

# Page 12^page=12
6.12 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Shared counters  Possible result: lost update!   Difficult to debug  Called a “race condition” hits = 0 + 1 read hits (0) hits = 0 + 1 read hits (0) T1 T2 hits = 1 hits = 0 time

6.13 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Objectives  To introduce the critical-section( 临界区 ) problem, whose solutions can be used to ensure the consistency of shared data  To present both software and hardware solutions of the critical-section problem （软件和硬件解决方法）  To introduce the concept of an atomic transaction and describe mechanisms to ensure atomicity （原子事务）

# Page 14^page=14
6.14 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Critical-Section ( 临界区 ) Critical resource （临界资源） :  The resource that must be accessed mutual excluded ( 互斥的 ). Critical section （临界区）： A segment of code in which the process is accessing critical resource Consider a set of concurrent processes{ P0,P1,…,Pn-1}. Each process has a segment of code, called a critical section, in which the process may be changing common variables, updating a table, writing a file, and so on.

# Page 15^page=15
6.15 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Critical-Section  To ensure the shared data to be modified mutual excluded, no more than one process can execute in its critical section at the same time. （不能有一个以上的进程同时在它的临界 区执行）  Each process must request permission to enter its critical section----entry section ( 进入区 )  And exit with the exit section ( 退出区 ). entry section exit section critical section remainder section

# Page 16^page=16
6.16 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Solution to Critical-Section Problem 1.Mutual Exclusion 互斥 (safety) - No more than one process can be in a critical section at any time. 2. Progress 有空让进 (liveness) - If no process is executing in its critical section and there exist some processes wishing to enter their critical section, then the selection of the processes that will enter the critical section next cannot be postponed indefinitely 3. Bounded Waiting 有限等待 - A bound must exist on the number of times that other processes are allowed to enter their critical sections after a process has made a request to enter its critical section and before that request is granted  Assume that each process executes at a nonzero speed  No assumption concerning relative speed of the N processes  Ideally we would like fairness （公平） as well  If two threads are both trying to enter a critical section, they have equal chances of success  … in practice, fairness is rarely guaranteed

# Page 19^page=19
6.19 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Solving the problem CSEnter() { while(inside) continue; inside = true; } #Critical section CSExit() { inside = false; }  A first idea:  Have a boolean flag, inside. Initially false. Code is unsafe: thread 0 could finish the while test when inside is false, but then 1 might call CSEnter() before 0 can set inside to true! CSEnter() { while(inside) continue; inside = true; } #Critical section CSExit() { inside = false; } T0: T1:

# Page 20^page=20
6.20 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Solving the problem: Take 2 CSEnter(int i) { inside[i] = true; while(inside[j]) continue; } #Critical section CSExit(int i) { Inside[i] = false; }  A different idea (assumes just two threads):  Have a boolean flag, inside[i]. Initially false. Code isn’t live: with bad luck, both threads could be looping, with 0 looking at 1, and 1 looking at 0 T0: T1: CSEnter(int j) { inside[j] = true; while(inside[i]) continue; } #Critical section CSExit(int j) { Inside[j] = false; }

# Page 21^page=21
6.21 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Solving the problem: Take 3 CSEnter(int i) { while(turn != i) continue; } #Critical section CSExit(int i) { turn = j; }  Another broken solution, for two threads  Have a turn variable, turn, initially 1. Code isn’t live: thread 1 can’t enter unless thread 0 did first, and vice-versa. But perhaps one thread needs to enter many times and the other fewer times, or not at all Other processing… T0: T1:

CSEnter() { while(inside) continue; inside = true; } #Critical section CSExit() { inside = false; } T0: T1: 唯一性标识

Initially false. Code isn’t live: with bad luck, both threads could be looping, with 0 looking at 1, and 1 looking at 0 T0: T1: CSEnter(int j) { inside[j] = true; while(inside[i]) continue; } #Critical section CSExit(int j) { Inside[j] = false; } 能不能访问

6.26 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Objectives  To introduce the critical-section( 临界区 ) problem, whose solutions can be used to ensure the consistency of shared data  To present both software and hardware solutions of the critical-section problem  To introduce the concept of an atomic transaction and describe mechanisms to ensure atomicity

# Page 27^page=27
6.27 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Peterson’s Solution  A classic software-based solution to the critical section problem.  Works for two processes.  The two processes share two variables:  int turn ： indicates whose turn it is to enter the critical section. ( 唯一性标识、能不能访问 )  Boolean flag[2] ： The flag array is used to indicate if a process is ready to enter the critical section. flag[i] = true implies that process Pi is ready! ( 想不想访问 )

# Page 28^page=28
6.28 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Peterson’s Algorithm fl ag[ i ] = TRUE; turn = j ; whi l e ( fl ag[ j ] && turn == j ) ; cri ti cal secti on fl ag[ i ] = FALSE; remai nder secti on fl ag[ j ] = TRUE; t urn = i ; whi l e ( fl ag[ i ] && t urn == i ) ; cri t i cal sect i on fl ag[ j ] = FALSE; remai nder sect i on Pi Pj 进程 Pi 与 Pj 共享两个参数： flag[]; ( 想不想访问 ) turn; ( 唯一性标识；能不能访 问 ) “礼貌的 Peterson” 思考： 如果不是只有两个进程呢？程序中需要改动吗？ 如果要改动，改动哪里？

# Page 29^page=29
6.29 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Dekker Algorithm  Set flag[]=false; turn=0; do{ flag[i]=true; while flag[j]==true{ if turn !=i{ flag[i]=false; while (turn != i); flag[i] =true; } } } 临界区 turn=1; flag[i]=false; “举手放手的 Dekker”

6.30 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Objectives  To introduce the critical-section( 临界区 ) problem, whose solutions can be used to ensure the consistency of shared data  To present both software and hardware solutions of the critical-section problem  To introduce the concept of an atomic transaction and describe mechanisms to ensure atomicity

# Page 31^page=31
6.31 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Synchronization Hardware  Many systems provide hardware support for critical section code. （许多系统为临界区代码提供硬件支持）  Hardware features can make programming task easier and improve system efficiency. （优点：简化编程任务， 提高系统效率）  Modern machines provide special atomic hardware instructions to solve critical section problem in a relatively simple manner. Atomic ( 原子 )= non-interruptable  通过特殊的内存访问电路  针对单处理器和多处理器

# Page 32^page=32
6.32 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Solution to Critical-section Problem Using Locks  In general, any solution to critical-section requires a lock ( 锁 ) in order to prevent race condition. do { Acquire lock #critical section Release lock #remainder section } while (true)  Two atomic algorithms:  TestAndSet  Swep

# Page 33^page=33
6.33 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Solution 1: TestAndSet Instruction  Definition: boolean TestAndSet (boolean *target) { boolean rv = *target; *target = TRUE; return rv: }

# Page 34^page=34
6.34 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Solution using TestAndSet whi l e TS( &l ock) ; l ock = FALSE; cri t i cal sect i on remai nder sect i on Shared boolean variable lock, initialized to false.

# Page 35^page=35
6.35 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Solution using TestAndSet Shared boolean variable lock, initialized to false. while (true) { while ( TestAndSet (&lock )) ; /* do nothing // critical section lock = FALSE; // remainder section } boolean TestAndSet (boolean *target) { boolean rv = *target; *target = TRUE; return rv; } false

# Page 36^page=36
6.36 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Solution 2: Swap Instruction  Definition: void Swap (boolean *a, boolean *b) { boolean temp = *a; *a = *b; *b = temp; }

# Page 37^page=37
6.37 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Solution using Swap key = TRUE; do { SWAP( &l ock, &key) ; } whi l e ( key) ; l ock = FALSE; cri t i cal sect i on remai nder sect i on Shared Boolean variable lock initialized to FALSE; Each process has a local Boolean variable key.

# Page 38^page=38
6.38 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Solution using Swap  Shared Boolean variable lock initialized to FALSE; Each process has a local Boolean variable key.  Solution: while (true) { key = TRUE; while ( key == TRUE) Swap (&lock, &key ); // critical section lock = FALSE; // remainder section }

# Page 39^page=39
6.39 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition 忙等待和无忙等待  忙等待 AcquireLoc(){ while(TestandSet(&lock)) } 临界区 Release(){ *lock=false; }

# Page 40^page=40
6.40 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition 忙等待和无忙等待  无忙等待 AcquireLoc(){ while(TestandSet(&lock)) add this TCB to wait queue q; } 临界区 Release() (){ *lock=false; remove one thread t from q; wakeup(t) }

# Page 41^page=41
6.41 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition 原子操作优点  适用于单处理器或者共享主存的多处理器 中任意数量的进程。  简单并且容易证明  可以用于支持多临界区

# Page 42^page=42
6.42 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition 原子操作的缺点  忙等待消耗处理器时间  当进程离开临界区并且多个进程在等待的 时候可能导致饥饿  死锁：  如果一个低优先级的进程拥有临界区，但同时 一个高优先级进程也有需求，那么高优先级进 程会获得处理器，但不得不等待临界区

# Page 43^page=43
6.43 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition 禁用中断  没有中断，没有上下文切换，因此没有并发  硬件将中断处理延迟到中断被启用之后  大多数现代计算机体系结构都提供指令来完成  进入临界区  禁用中断  离开临界区  开启中断

# Page 44^page=44
6.44 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition 禁用中断缺点  一旦中断被禁用 , 线程就无法被停止  整个系统都会为该线程停下来  可能导致其他线程处于饥饿状态  如果临界区可以任意长：  无法限制响应中断所需的时间  只能禁用该 CPU 自己的中断指令，无法控 制其他 CPU 的中断指令，仍然存在隐患

# Page 45^page=45
6.45 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition An example  当队列中有 n 个进程 p0,p2,...pn-1, 如何管理他们的 临界区？用原语写出一段访问临界资源的代码。 do { waiting[i]=true; key = true; while (waiting[i] && key) key=TestAndSet (&lock); // critical section j=(i+1) % n; while((j != i)) && !waiting[j] j=(j+1) % n; if(j == i) lock=false; else waiting[j]=false; }while(true) ( 想不想访问 ) ( 唯一性标识 ) ( 能不能访问 )

# Page 46^page=46
6.46 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition 临界区小结  锁是解决临界区问题的常用方法：  互斥可以使用锁来实现  通常需要一定等级的硬件支持  常用的三种实现方法：  禁用中断（仅限于单处理器）  软件方法（复杂）  硬件方法（原子操作指令，单处理器或多处理器均可）  可选的实现内容：  有忙等待  无忙等待

# Page 53^page=53
6.53 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Semaphore  A Semaphore is a special integer variable （特殊的整数 变量） , it can be accessed only through two standard atomic operations:  Wait() and Signal()  Originally called P() and V()  Wait(S) { while S <= 0 ; // busy wait S--; }  signal (S) { S++; }

# Page 54^page=54
6.54 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Semaphore Implementation  The main disadvantage of the above semaphore definition is busy waiting which wastes CPU cycles.  Spinlock （自旋锁）  Process spins while waiting for the lock.  In a multiprocessor system, spinlock is often used to protect a short code. （通常用于保护短代码）  Note that applications may spend lots of time in critical sections and therefore this is not a good solution. （不是 一个好的解决方案）

# Page 55^page=55
6.55 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Semaphore Implementation with no Busy waiting  To overcome busy waiting, we can modify the implementation of semaphore S and the definition of wait() and signal(). （修改 wait() 和 signal() 的定义）  Now that a semaphore is a struct.  a value (of type integer)  a waiting queue  Two operations:  block – place the process invoking the operation on the appropriate waiting queue.  wakeup – remove one of processes in the waiting queue and place it in the ready queue.

# Page 56^page=56
6.56 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Semaphore Implementation with no Busy waiting (Cont.)  Implementation of wait: wait (S){ value--; if (value < 0) { add this process to waiting queue block(); } }  Implementation of signal: Signal (S){ value++; if (value <= 0) { remove a process P from the waiting queue wakeup(P); } }

# Page 57^page=57
6.57 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Semaphore as General Synchronization Tool  Counting semaphore  integer value can range over an unrestricted domain  Can be used to control access to a given resource consisting of a finite number of instances.  Binary semaphore  integer value can range only between 0 and 1; can be simpler to implement  Also known as mutex locks （互斥锁）  Provides mutual exclusion  Semaphore S; // initialized to 1  wait (S); Critical Section signal (S);

# Page 150^page=150
6.15 4 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Multiple Notifications  notify() selects an arbitrary thread T from the wait set  If there are multiple threads in wait set and more than one condition for which to wait, it is possible to have another deadlock. （如果等待集中有多个线程，并且需 要等待的条件不止一个，则可能再次发生死锁）  例：有 5 个线程 T1 ， T2 ， T3 ， T4 ， T5 ，一个共享 变量 turn ， 5 个线程都需要调用 dowork() 方法，规定只 有编号与 turn 相同的线程才能进入 dowork() 。假定 turn=3 ， T1 ， T2 ， T4 在等待集中， T3 在调用 dowork() 。当 T3 完成了它的工作，它把 turn 设为 4 ，并 调用 notify() 。 notify() 从等待集中任意选取一个线程， 假设选中了 T2 ，但当 T2 恢复运行后检查条件时，发现 turn 并不是它的值，于是 T2 再次调用 wait(), 进入等待集。 接下来，如果 T3 ， T5 调用 dowork() ，也会进入等待集。 这样， 5 个线程都进入了等待集，形成了死锁。
